name: K'Sante
playstyleSummary: K'Sante is a tanky fighter who marks enemies with his abilities for bonus damage on his next attack, then can flip the switch with his ultimate into a more aggressive, higher-damage form at the cost of his defenses. Most of the game he plays like a sturdy front-liner setting up damage for himself, but his ultimate turns a fight into an all-or-nothing gamble. He rewards players who like a kit that changes shape mid-fight.
strengths: Ntofo Strikes builds toward a shockwave that pulls enemies in, great for punishing overextended targets.
Path Maker reduces incoming damage while charging and can knock back and stun enemies.
Footwork gives mobility and a shield to himself or an ally.
All Out transforms him into a much higher-damage, higher-healing form for finishing fights.
weaknesses: All Out lowers his defenses in exchange for offense, making him more vulnerable while transformed.
Needs to land an ability before attacking to get the bonus damage from Dauntless Instinct.
Ntofo Strikes needs two stacks before its shockwave pull is available.
Complex kit with abilities that behave differently once All Out is active, making him hard to master.

abilities.name: Dauntless Instinct
abilities.howToUseIt: Your abilities mark enemies, and your next Attack against a marked target deals bonus damage — try to land an ability before attacking for extra damage. Once you go All Out from your ultimate, this bonus increases further on both Attacks and Abilities.

abilities.name: Ntofo Strikes
abilities.howToUseIt: Hit an enemy to slow them and gain a stack; at two stacks, your next cast fires a shockwave that pulls enemies in — use it to set up fights or punish enemies who get close. While All Out, its cooldown is shorter.

abilities.name: Path Maker
abilities.howToUseIt: Charge it up to reduce incoming damage, then release to dash forward, knocking back and stunning enemies in your path — great for engaging or protecting yourself while charging. While All Out, it deals more damage but no longer knocks back or stuns, so use it differently in that state.

abilities.name: Footwork
abilities.howToUseIt: Dash for a shield on your own, or target an ally for increased range with both of you gaining a shield — use it to close distance, escape, or protect a teammate. While All Out, its cooldown is shorter and it grants extra speed.

abilities.name: All Out
abilities.howToUseIt: Knock an enemy back through any wall behind them, then transform into a more aggressive form and dash after them — you'll deal more damage and heal more, but your defenses drop, so commit carefully.

earlyGamePlan: In lane, use Ntofo Strikes to poke and build stacks, saving the two-stack shockwave to punish enemies who walk up carelessly. Play a normal tanky role early, using Path Maker and Footwork for engage and protection, and save All Out for when you can commit to a fight, since it lowers your defenses in exchange for damage.
quickTips: Land an ability before attacking whenever possible — Dauntless Instinct rewards you with bonus damage on marked targets.
Build two stacks of Ntofo Strikes to unlock its shockwave pull, which is great for punishing overextended enemies.
Use Path Maker's charge time to shield yourself from incoming damage before committing to the dash.
Remember All Out trades your defenses for offense — don't activate it if you're not ready to fully commit to the fight.
Use Footwork on an ally to protect them with a shield while repositioning together.
whatToExpect: An enemy K'Sante marks you with his abilities before attacking for bonus damage, so expect extra damage right after he lands a spell. Watch for two stacks of Ntofo Strikes, since the next hit pulls you toward him. His ultimate, All Out, knocks you back through terrain and then transforms him into a more aggressive, higher-damage form with lower defenses — since his defenses drop while All Out, focusing him down during that window can be effective.

glossary.definition: Dealing damage from a safe distance to slowly wear an enemy down.
Building up multiple instances of an effect — here, charges of Ntofo Strikes.
Starting a fight by committing first, often with crowd control or a dash.
